meta.title: Saule — Adaptive Intelligence for Life
meta.description: The Saule White Paper documents the philosophy, research, architecture, psychology, and engineering behind Saule.

footer.warning: The Saule White Paper is currently in early definition. This public document evolves as research progress is recorded.

early_access.title: Subscribe to the build log
early_access.description: We document our thinking, research notes, and design decisions here. Subscribe to be notified when new chapters are locked or interactive sandboxes open.
early_access.success_desc: Thank you for subscribing. We will reach out when the initial interactive sandbox is ready.

dashboard.title: Saule White Paper v1.0
dashboard.subtitle: The Semantic Memory Layer (SML)
dashboard.status_desc: Refining and detailing the white paper draft
dashboard.preface_body_1: “The Saule application will be developed alongside the Saule White Paper and in accordance with its principles.”
dashboard.preface_body_2: This document does more than describe Saule. It is Saule’s intellectual, ethical, and engineering foundation: the founding source that defines why Saule exists, which human problem it seeks to address, the boundaries within which it will develop, and the principles from which it must not depart.
dashboard.preface_body_3: Saule is being developed in response to profound problems of modern life: mental clutter, decision fatigue, forgotten intentions, unfinished plans, and the loss of connection people can feel with their own lives. For that reason, Saule’s technical development cannot be reduced to adding features. Every feature must be grounded in how it touches human life, what burden it reduces, what awareness it fosters, and the responsibility with which it will be used.

workspace.not_written: This chapter has not been written yet.

workspace.open_questions_intro: These questions are intentionally left open at this stage. They define the research paths that later chapters must answer before Saule is implemented.
workspace.draft_mode_desc: This chapter is currently in draft mode.

v1.title: Saule White Paper v1.0
v1.purpose: A Living Memory for People, Teams, and Institutions
v1.intro_p1: Artificial intelligence is becoming a commodity. Reasoning models improve month by month, prices drop, and switching between providers is easier than ever. But something far more valuable is being lost. Not intelligence. Memory.
v1.intro_p2: Human knowledge, organizational context, relationships, decisions, and experiences are fragmented across dozens of digital tools. Every platform stores information, but none preserves meaning over time.
v1.intro_p3: Saule introduces a new software category: The Semantic Memory Layer (SML). Instead of building a new AI model, Saule builds an independent memory layer that preserves semantic context across individuals, teams, and communities—no matter which AI model powers it. Intelligence may change. Memory does not.

access_page.title: Saule Early Access
access_page.subtitle: The application is currently being developed under an invitation-only model.
access_page.why_text: Saule touches sensitive areas such as personal memory, attention, decisions, relationships, and mental load. Therefore, a controlled application and invitation model will be used instead of public registration in the early stages.
access_page.process_item2: Fill out the early access form
access_page.process_item4: If approved, an invitation link is sent
access_page.process_item5: You participate in the feedback process
access_page.expectations_item2: Saule does not offer diagnosis or treatment
access_page.expectations_item4: Honest feedback is expected from participants
access_page.expectations_item5: User data and privacy boundaries are primary
access_page.form_reason: Why do you want to try Saule?
access_page.form_struggle: In which area do you struggle the most?
access_page.form_takeaway: Can you write a takeaway or a sentence from the white paper that stuck in your mind?
access_page.form_feedback: Are you open to giving feedback?
access_page.form_consent: I understand that Saule is not clinical support

home_page.hero_desc: AI models constantly evolve, but memory remains constant. Saule is an independent Semantic Memory Layer that preserves your mental load, decisions, and intentions, understanding you over time regardless of which AI assistant you use.
home_page.section_saule_desc: Saule is not split into separate product worlds. Work, creativity, relationships, decisions, and daily life are different contexts of the same human being; Saule approaches them through one ethical and design spine.
home_page.book_desc: Saule’s philosophical, psychological, ethical, and engineering foundation is clearly defined here.
home_page.principles_desc: Saule aims to preserve contextual integrity rather than just generating immediate outputs.
principles: Model Independence: Not locked into any LLM provider; keeps you in control.
Seamless Context: Transforms fragmented digital footprints into a coherent, holistic story.
Active Memory: Positions humans not merely as prompt sources, but as the subjects of memory.
Silent Assistant: Doesn't constantly wait for directions; learns in the background and acts at the right moment.
home_page.problem_desc: The problem for modern individuals and institutions is not a lack of information. It is a fragmented memory crisis. Every platform remembers a small piece of the story, but none knows the whole story.
home_page.solution_desc: Instead of building a new AI model, Saule builds an independent memory layer that preserves semantic context across individuals, teams, and communities—no matter which AI model powers it.
home_page.footer_cta: Intelligence may change. Memory does not.
